---
title: "The Data"
subtitle: "Three-country macro panels with pseudo-real-time discipline"
---

## Data sources

Each country's panel combines domestic national statistics, central bank data, and global macro indicators from FRED. Every variable has a **publication lag** --- the delay between the end of the reference month and when the data actually becomes available.

| Country | Domestic source | Central bank | Targets |
|---------|----------------|--------------|---------|
| **Norway** | SSB (Statistics Norway) | Norges Bank | CPI, industrial production, retail sales, unemployment |
| **Canada** | Statistics Canada | Bank of Canada | CPI, industrial production, retail sales, unemployment |
| **Sweden** | SCB (Statistics Sweden) | Riksbank | CPI, industrial production, unemployment |

Global covariates (from FRED): Brent crude, S&P 500, Fed funds rate, US CPI, Euro area GDP, VIX, Global EPU.

Country-specific covariates include exchange rates, policy rates, house prices, credit, exports, and imports.

## Pseudo-real-time discipline

At each forecast origin, we only use data that would have been **actually available** on that date. This means applying publication lags: CPI is available 10 days after month-end, unemployment after 30 days, Euro area GDP after 90 days.

For an observation from month *m*:

> The observation is available when: **end-of-month(m) + publication lag <= t**

This prevents future information leakage. Publication lags range from 0 days (policy rate, known in real time) to 90 days (Euro area GDP). Daily series are aggregated to monthly averages; quarterly series are forward-filled.

## Key covariates by country

The search agent selects different covariates for each country, reflecting the distinct economic structures:

| Country | Selected covariates | Interpretation |
|---------|-------------------|----------------|
| **Norway** | S&P 500, policy rate, fed funds, NOK/USD | Oil economy sensitive to global risk and USD-denominated commodities |
| **Canada** | Policy rate, Euro area GDP | Large trading partner exposure, rate-sensitive economy |
| **Sweden** | Policy rate, SEK/USD, house prices, credit | Domestic credit cycle and exchange rate pass-through |

## Variable count

| Country | Target variables | Total panel variables |
|---------|-----------------|---------------------|
| Norway | 4 | 18 |
| Canada | 4 | 18 |
| Sweden | 3 | 16 |

Sweden has fewer targets because retail sales data does not have sufficient history for the validation era (2006--2015).
